title: Anniversaries

jokes.text: And the groom wore a butt so perfect it could make an angel hang itself.
jokes.setup: Reading a formal wedding announcement with flowery romantic language
jokes.punchline: Escalates to absurd hyperbole about Ben's attractiveness causing celestial suicide
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from romantic to absurdist. 'Angel hang itself' is wonderfully unexpected and quotable. Strong craft in the misdirection.

jokes.text: You wrote this, didn't you? - I did. - They cut it way down. - Thank God.
jokes.setup: Overly flowery wedding announcement reading
jokes.punchline: Reveals Leslie wrote the excessive prose and Ben's relief it was edited
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Leslie's over-the-top enthusiasm and Ben's pragmatism

jokes.text: It's like... Wha-a-a-at?
jokes.setup: Leslie describing Ben's surprise face
jokes.punchline: Her exaggerated impression of his dopey expression
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy through Leslie's physical impression, shows her affection

jokes.text: She will make that stupid face!
jokes.setup: Ben's confidence about surprising Leslie first
jokes.punchline: His competitive enthusiasm about making her look 'stupid'
jokes.explanation: Shows Ben's competitive side in their relationship dynamic

jokes.text: the Richard and Lois Wallenberg Foundation Foundation... dedicated to the idea that all human beings deserve a chance to hear about foundations.
jokes.setup: Standard NPR-style foundation acknowledgments
jokes.punchline: Foundation Foundation and circular mission statement about hearing about foundations
jokes.explanation: Clever satire of NPR funding announcements with recursive absurdity

jokes.text: I believe that is the Eagleton-Pawnee merger, n'est-ce pas?
jokes.setup: Discussion of town merger
jokes.punchline: Pretentious correction with unnecessary French phrase
jokes.explanation: Establishes August's pretentious character through unnecessary French

jokes.text: I'd like to apologize to our listeners for my co-host's sudden and violent outburst just now.
jokes.setup: August's mild correction about the merger name
jokes.punchline: Derry dramatically overreacting by calling it a 'violent outburst'
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan exaggeration that establishes the co-hosts' dynamic

jokes.text: Leslie, thought... - For your thoughts. I said it. Absorb the aggression, Derry.
jokes.setup: Show's catchphrase setup
jokes.punchline: Leslie jumping in and August's passive-aggressive coaching
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamics and Leslie's eagerness disrupting the format

jokes.text: such a big, quiet, arcane ball of fun
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to compliment the merged radio shows
jokes.punchline: Accidentally describes it as boring with 'quiet' and 'arcane'
jokes.explanation: Leslie's diplomatic language accidentally revealing the show's dullness

jokes.text: I haven't seen Pawneeans this mad since Frankie's discontinued their pizza-stuffed-crust pizza.
jokes.setup: Describing town anger levels
jokes.punchline: Comparison to anger over discontinued pizza-stuffed-crust pizza
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist food reference that feels very Parks and Rec specific

jokes.text: It was a pizza whose crust was stuffed with little pizzas. And the crust of those little pizzas was made of chocolate.
jokes.setup: Already absurd pizza-stuffed-crust concept
jokes.punchline: Escalates to recursive pizza-within-pizza with chocolate crust
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and absurdist food creation that's quotably ridiculous

jokes.text: Scoff.
jokes.setup: Leslie defending the merger
jokes.punchline: August literally saying the word 'scoff' instead of scoffing
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character-specific pretentiousness. Single word does massive comedic work.

jokes.text: Did you just say the word 'scoff'?
jokes.setup: August saying 'Scoff' literally
jokes.punchline: Derry's confused reaction highlighting the absurdity
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat that highlights the previous joke's absurdity

jokes.text: This twerp has a name. And it's August N'gutu Liebowitz-Clementine.
jokes.setup: Being called a nameless twerp
jokes.punchline: Overly elaborate pretentious name with multiple hyphens
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment through absurdly pretentious name

jokes.text: Like, was it a hypnosis accident or something, where they put Gayle under and made her fall in love with Larry and never said the magic word to snap her out of it?
jokes.setup: Wondering how Larry landed his beautiful wife
jokes.punchline: Elaborate hypnosis theory to explain the mismatch
jokes.explanation: Creative absurdist explanation for the Larry/Gayle dynamic

jokes.text: Um, I work here, so, legally, you can adopt me.
jokes.setup: Question about adopting pets
jokes.punchline: April offers herself for adoption with fake legal reasoning
jokes.explanation: Perfect April deadpan delivery with absurd logic. Very character-specific.

jokes.text: So, like a banana made of spiders?
jokes.setup: Looking at children's drawing of desired dog
jokes.punchline: April's horrifying interpretation of the drawing
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly horrifying imagery that's both specific and absurd. Very quotable.

jokes.text: I've said it before and I'll say it again, children are terrible artists.
jokes.setup: Bad children's drawing
jokes.punchline: April's blunt assessment delivered as universal truth
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor delivered in April's characteristic blunt style

jokes.text: And artists are crooks.
jokes.setup: Criticizing children's artistic ability
jokes.punchline: Extends criticism to all artists as criminals
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing April's cynical worldview

jokes.text: Why lie? I'm shopping. Back in an hour.
jokes.setup: April looking for Donna at work
jokes.punchline: Donna's brutally honest out-of-office note
jokes.explanation: Perfect Donna attitude - unapologetically honest about shirking work

jokes.text: No, I mean Eagletonians are human garbage. And we should throw them in a dumpster. Kapow!
jokes.setup: Discussing trash problems from merger
jokes.punchline: Caller reveals they mean people are garbage, adds 'Kapow!'
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about town rivalry with absurd 'Kapow!' ending

jokes.text: Well, that's what the merger is. Oh. Yeah, then I hate the merger.
jokes.setup: Caller likes merger but hates Eagleton being forced to join
jokes.punchline: Realizes this IS the merger and immediately changes position
jokes.explanation: Good logic flip showing caller's confusion about the merger

jokes.text: It's Andy! Hey. I'm on the radio!
jokes.setup: Anonymous caller praising Leslie
jokes.punchline: Andy blowing his own cover with childlike excitement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy moment - well-intentioned but can't contain his excitement

jokes.text: And I talked last. Good day.
jokes.setup: Radio show ending
jokes.punchline: August's petty victory claim about speaking last
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing August's petty competitiveness

jokes.text: Because I want to blow up Donna's desk so I can teach her a lesson about what happens when you skip work.
jokes.setup: April asking about a land mine
jokes.punchline: Wants to use military ordnance for workplace discipline
jokes.explanation: Perfect April solution - completely disproportionate violence for minor workplace issue

jokes.text: Great first date spot.
jokes.setup: April writing reviews under Kyle's name
jokes.punchline: Reviewing a funeral home as a date location
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor with perfect economy. Very April to find death romantic.

jokes.text: Stopped by to adopt a dog, but the employee, Donna Meagle, was nowhere to be found. Thanks for nothing, Donna Meagle. Booooo!
jokes.setup: April writing fake negative reviews
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic fake review with extended 'boo'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing April's passive-aggressive revenge tactics

jokes.text: With five 'O's.
jokes.setup: Dramatic 'Booooo' in fake review
jokes.punchline: April specifying the exact number of O's for emphasis
jokes.explanation: Good detail that shows April's commitment to her petty revenge

jokes.text: I'm about to hunt you down.
jokes.setup: Donna reading negative review
jokes.punchline: Threatens physical violence over anonymous internet comment
jokes.explanation: Shows Donna's fierce pride and willingness to escalate conflicts

jokes.text: Andy, this is a picture of us. This is a picture of an elderly couple.
jokes.setup: Andy commenting on how old Leslie looks in photo
jokes.punchline: Leslie revealing it's not even them in the picture
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Andy's confusion and Leslie's exasperation

jokes.text: Whoo! I'm getting the epiphany sweats.
jokes.setup: Leslie getting excited about the elderly couple idea
jokes.punchline: Physical description of her excitement as 'epiphany sweats'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie-ism combining physical comedy with her intense enthusiasm

jokes.text: Just thinking about the new Star Wars sequel. I'm afraid they're gonna rely too heavily on CGI. And I'm carrying it all in my shoulders.
jokes.setup: Ben explaining his massage tension
jokes.punchline: Stress about fictional movie affecting him physically
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben nerd anxiety translated into physical symptoms. Very character-specific.

jokes.text: Larry, would you like to get a couples massage with me?
jokes.setup: Ben needs someone for his couples massage
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly asking Larry to be his massage partner
jokes.explanation: Good cringe setup for awkward situation between male coworkers

jokes.text: Last time we scared an old person, Larry had a heart attack. That was awesome. He farted so much.
jokes.setup: Concerns about scaring elderly people
jokes.punchline: Andy's enthusiasm about Larry's medical emergency and farting
jokes.explanation: Dark humor showing Andy's childlike delight in others' misfortune

jokes.text: Oh, no, you didn't win any money, you won something better than money. A golden anniversary celebration. So no money.
jokes.setup: Elderly couple expecting money prize
jokes.punchline: Leslie's awkward oversell followed by blunt confirmation
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Leslie's desperation and the couple's disappointment

jokes.text: I got football cards, and a travel mouthwash.
jokes.setup: Everyone giving elderly couple money
jokes.punchline: Andy's random non-monetary offerings
jokes.explanation: Good Andy randomness - typical pocket contents of a child-man

jokes.text: I'm about to come down on this dude like Thor's hammer Mjolnir. I'm about to go Mjolnir on his ass!
jokes.setup: Donna angry about negative review
jokes.punchline: Elaborate Thor reference with pronunciation and repetition
jokes.explanation: Shows Donna's pop culture knowledge and escalating anger, but slightly overwrought

jokes.text: Dear frozen yogurt, you are the celery of desserts. Be ice cream or be nothing. Zero stars.
jokes.setup: Ron writing complaint letters
jokes.punchline: Harsh but poetic condemnation of frozen yogurt
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson line - poetic, harsh, and philosophically consistent with his character. Highly quotable with great metaphor.

jokes.text: I watched Enchanted three times to get all the details right. And then another two times, because that movie is amazing.
jokes.setup: Ben preparing Leslie's surprise
jokes.punchline: Admits to watching Enchanted five times total, justifying the extra viewings
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ben's romantic side and secret movie preferences

jokes.text: Good sir, tally ho. God, that... from the movie! That is definitely from the movie.
jokes.setup: Ben trying to recreate romantic movie moments
jokes.punchline: Awkward attempt at period language followed by self-conscious justification
jokes.explanation: Good cringe showing Ben's awkward romanticism and movie obsession

jokes.text: They're like the old version of iTunes. We're like the new version of iTunes, baby. We're gonna be here forever. Oh, my God. I'm gonna die someday.
jokes.setup: Tom dismissing old people as obsolete
jokes.punchline: Technology metaphor leads to sudden mortality realization
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from tech metaphor to existential crisis. Very Tom to make it about himself.

jokes.text: Hey, no touching. This is not your birthday.
jokes.setup: Elderly couple posing for recreation photo
jokes.punchline: Wife refusing physical contact with specific condition
jokes.explanation: Good cringe establishing the couple's dysfunction with specific birthday rule

jokes.text: Don't give him any water. He does this annoying thing where every time he takes a drink of water he goes, 'Ah...'
jokes.setup: Husband asking for water before TV appearance
jokes.punchline: Wife's petty complaint about his drinking sound
jokes.explanation: Good petty marital complaint showing their dysfunction through tiny details

jokes.text: She does this annoying thing where every time she takes a drink of water she is an old nag.
jokes.setup: Wife's complaint about his water drinking sounds
jokes.punchline: Husband's much harsher counter-complaint
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the husband's nastier approach to their bickering

jokes.text: Just took some ludes to kind of smooth it out, so I'm right in that sweet spot. Got about 20 minutes before I either get really tired or really horny, so let's roll.
jokes.setup: Doug taking uppers before TV appearance
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of taking Quaaludes with graphic timeline
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about elderly drug use with inappropriate oversharing

jokes.text: You look like a leather glove got caught in a lawnmower.
jokes.setup: Doug asking if he looks okay for TV
jokes.punchline: Wife's vivid and cruel description of his appearance
jokes.explanation: Excellent vivid imagery that's both specific and cruel. Very quotable insult.

jokes.text: You tried to adopt a cat last week and I turned you down, because I thought it was too sad for you to have six cats. Then you tried to blow up my spot online. You're fired.
jokes.setup: Donna confronting Kyle about the review
jokes.punchline: Reveals she rejected his cat adoption for his own good, then fires him
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Donna was actually looking out for Kyle, then nuclear response

jokes.text: You're not my boss, I work for the city attorney's office. You're fired.
jokes.setup: Kyle pointing out Donna can't fire him
jokes.punchline: Donna ignoring logic and firing him anyway
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Donna's refusal to accept logical limitations

jokes.text: Who's it going to? Canada. It says, 'Dear Canada, you.'
jokes.setup: April asking about postage for Ron's letter
jokes.punchline: Ron addressing an entire country with incomplete insult
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist humor - Ron insulting an entire nation with minimal effort. Very quotable.

jokes.text: I had no prospects, and Dougie here had an alcohol problem. Plus the Cold War was happening, and frankly, we all thought we were gonna die.
jokes.setup: Asked about romantic reason for marriage
jokes.punchline: Brutally unromantic list of practical desperation
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor deflating romantic expectations with historical context

jokes.text: Well, I figured I could really tick off my parents by shacking up with some Pawnee trash.
jokes.setup: Doug's reason for marriage
jokes.punchline: Admits marrying wife purely to spite parents and insult her town
jokes.explanation: Dark humor showing how awful their marriage foundation was, advancing the town rivalry theme

jokes.text: I slept with Sharon in 1979.
jokes.setup: Interview going off the rails
jokes.punchline: Casual confession of decades-old adultery on live radio
jokes.explanation: Great economy in devastating revelation. Perfect escalation of their dysfunction.

jokes.text: I used to get out of the shower in front of your brother on purpose.
jokes.setup: Doug's adultery confession
jokes.punchline: Rosie's counter-revelation about inappropriate behavior with in-law
jokes.explanation: Good escalation matching his confession with equally inappropriate behavior

jokes.text: They eat, they sleep, they complain, and they watch Family Feud. Oh, my God, I wanna be an old person.
jokes.setup: Tom listing boring activities of elderly people
jokes.punchline: Suddenly realizes this sounds appealing to him
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tom's recognition that leisure sounds great

jokes.text: Veganism is the sad result of a morally corrupt mind. Reconsider your life.
jokes.setup: Ron's letter to vegan restaurant
jokes.punchline: Philosophical condemnation followed by blunt advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron philosophy delivered with characteristic bluntness. Highly quotable and very character-specific.

jokes.text: A vegan is gonna physically attack me? They're basically made of glass.
jokes.setup: Question about consequences of confronting vegans
jokes.punchline: Ron's dismissive assessment of vegan physical capabilities
jokes.explanation: Excellent Ron observation with vivid metaphor about dietary weakness

jokes.text: It was a review of a funeral home that said 'Great first date spot.'
jokes.setup: Donna explaining how she knew April wrote the fake reviews
jokes.punchline: Callback to April's dark humor about funeral homes
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Donna's detective skills and April's consistent darkness

jokes.text: Your lipstick looks weird. You're gonna want to shut your mouth right now.
jokes.setup: April and Donna agreeing to be direct with each other
jokes.punchline: April immediately testing boundaries, Donna immediately shutting it down
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate test of their new honesty policy with swift consequences.

jokes.text: Craig Middlebrooks. Samantha in the boardroom, Miranda in the bedroom. I know it's not ideal, but it's who I am.
jokes.setup: Youth committee introductions
jokes.punchline: Oversharing about personality duality with Sex and the City reference
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment through inappropriate oversharing and pop culture reference

jokes.text: Donna said she might be able to get Ginuwine. That's given me a reason to live.
jokes.setup: Describing Unity Concert plans
jokes.punchline: Dramatic life-changing impact of potential R&B singer booking
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Craig's dramatic personality and music obsession

jokes.text: I don't... I don't... I don't even... You know what I mean?
jokes.setup: Leslie hearing the Unity Concert idea
jokes.punchline: Complete inability to form words due to excitement
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy through Leslie's emotional overwhelm, shows her enthusiasm

jokes.text: That means she loves it so much she can't speak.
jokes.setup: Leslie making incomprehensible excited noises
jokes.punchline: April's matter-of-fact translation of Leslie's condition
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan interpretation showing April's understanding of Leslie's patterns

jokes.text: Oh, my eff-ing God.
jokes.setup: Leslie seeing Ben's elaborate anniversary surprise
jokes.punchline: Leslie censoring herself while completely overwhelmed
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate censoring showing Leslie's excitement and propriety

jokes.text: I had a guy at the model store make a replica. He's a bigger Game of Thrones fan than you are.
jokes.setup: Leslie giving Ben the Iron Throne replica
jokes.punchline: Reveals the maker's superior fandom credentials
jokes.explanation: Good detail about nerd hierarchy and Leslie's thorough gift research

jokes.text: When you play the game of thrones, you win or you die. Yes. No. Yes. No!
jokes.setup: Leslie quoting Game of Thrones on the Iron Throne
jokes.punchline: Ben's attempts to correct her quote getting increasingly frantic
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy showing Ben's nerd precision vs Leslie's enthusiasm

jokes.text: Our starship is in trouble. Okay, Leslie, listen. If you're not... if you don't know what you're talking about...
jokes.setup: Leslie continuing to mix up franchises
jokes.punchline: Ben's polite but desperate attempt to stop her butchering references
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Ben's nerd frustration with Leslie's franchise confusion

tmdb_overview: Leslie's efforts to take down the wall separating Eagleton and Pawnee yield unexpected results; Ben and Tom meet with possible sponsors for the concert; Ron finds a new hobby.
insight: Character comedy carries a middling 78-score episode with 66 jokes across 42 minutes.
